arrival_observations: The school gate is already open. A hand-painted sign for Colegio Monte Araucaria hangs beside a wall patched in two different shades of blue.
The courtyard is louder than the street: sneakers on concrete, a canteen shutter rattling, someone laughing before the joke reaches you.
No one pauses because you arrived. The morning has been moving for a while.

classmates.name: Bia
classmates.visual_identity: yellow hair clip, green backpack, sticker album under one arm
classmates.routine: trades duplicate stickers near the canteen line, then checks whether anyone saw her older cousin at the gate

classmates.name: Caio
classmates.visual_identity: rolled uniform sleeves, red futsal wristband, scuffed black sneakers
classmates.routine: keeps a bottle-cap goal marked beside the bench and calls fouls that only he noticed

classmates.name: Luan
classmates.visual_identity: oversized glasses, blue notebook full of folded loose pages
classmates.routine: copies tomorrow's homework from a friend while insisting he is only checking the date

classmates.name: Marina
classmates.visual_identity: braided ribbon, patched shoulder bag, tiny radio keychain
classmates.routine: waits by the classroom door and hums the chorus leaking from the keychain speaker

classroom_observations: The classroom smells like chalk dust, floor wax, and rain that has not decided whether to start.
Desks scrape into old territories. Some seats already seem claimed even without names on them.
relationship_memory: A new student noticing who makes room, who pulls away, and which friendships keep moving when no one is watching.

recess_basketball.memory_intent: A loose recess game where watching, joining, or stepping away can become funny, awkward, warm, or tense without turning the moment into a score.
setup_observations: By recess, the basketball hoop has a line of chipped paint under it and three different arguments about who is actually on whose team.
The ball sounds heavier than it looks when it hits the concrete. Someone has already declared the next point worth two, for reasons no one accepts.

choices.label: Watch from the low wall
choices.outcome: Caio attempts a no-look pass to impress the entire courtyard and sends the ball into a stack of plastic cups near the canteen. He points at the wind, though there is no wind.
choices.later_behavior: Later, Caio retells the disaster just loudly enough for your side of the bench, leaving a pause where shared laughter could fit.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: Caio starts performing mistakes like they might become private jokes if someone notices them twice.

choices.label: Join when the ball rolls over
choices.outcome: The first pass reaches you at the wrong height. Your attempt becomes half catch, half self-defense, and the ball pops loose while everyone decides whether to laugh with you or at gravity.
choices.later_behavior: Later, Bia shifts her sticker album away from the scuffed court line and says the next team needs someone who can survive passes.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: Bia's teasing leaves a place inside it, as if the joke only works if you are still nearby.

choices.label: Leave for the canteen shade
choices.outcome: You step away just as teams are being counted again. A complaint follows your back, then gets swallowed by the canteen line and the smell of fried pastel.
choices.later_behavior: Later, Luan notices the quiet spot you chose and keeps one end of the bench unclaimed, while Caio counts teams without looking over.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: Luan treats the choice to step away as information rather than a problem to fix.

bia.behavior: Bia keeps the sticker album close unless a shared desk gives her a reason to risk the corner of it.

caio.behavior: Caio saves his loudest commentary for people who laugh at the same moment he does.

luan.behavior: Luan shares paper before he shares certainty.

marina.behavior: Marina makes room through small gestures before she says anything direct.

bia:caio.behavior: Bia and Caio can argue over a game and still end up near the same bench.

seats.label: Front row near the teacher's desk
seats.consequence: Luan slides his notebook halfway between the desks when the teacher asks for yesterday's date. The margin has three different answers and one careful unsure dot.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: At recess, Luan keeps the notebook open on the bench beside him, leaving enough space for another person without waving anyone over.

seats.label: Window seat beside the faded curtain
seats.consequence: Marina catches the curtain before it snaps against your face. For the rest of roll call, the two of you share the same strip of moving light.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: At recess, Marina waits a little longer near the classroom door, then tilts her radio keychain so the chorus reaches your side of the hallway.

seats.label: Middle row between two conversations
seats.consequence: Bia parks her sticker album on the corner of your desk when the teacher turns to the board. One shiny duplicate stays there longer than an accident would.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: At recess, Bia leaves one duplicate sticker face up on the bench between you and the canteen line, close enough to notice but not close enough to demand an answer.

seats.label: Back row near the loose fan switch
seats.consequence: Caio taps the fan switch with a pencil and looks innocent when the blades cough awake. The first laugh reaches your desk before the teacher turns around.

relationship_effect.behavior_feedback: At recess, Caio points his bottle-cap goal toward your side of the bench before pretending he only needed more space.

independent_social_events.behavior_feedback: Across the courtyard, Bia rescues a sticker from Caio's bottle-cap goal line. Caio complains to everyone except her, and a minute later the cap goal has moved two tiles away from her album.

reliability_profile.pattern: usually notices real details first, then adds confidence before the detail is finished
learnable_clues: Bia checks the gate for her older cousin before anyone else mentions older students.
Bia rescues the sticker before Caio admits the bottle-cap goal moved.
Bia names the wrong storage room door, then corrects herself only when Marina's radio keychain crackles nearby.

discovery.place: canteen shade beside the corridor to the storage rooms
requires_repeated_attention: notice Bia's gate-checking routine on arrival
notice the Bia and Caio sticker-goal argument
linger after the basketball choice instead of saving immediately

rumors.memory_intent: The first rumor should feel like overhearing a school myth halfway through, rewarding a player who remembers small contradictions without turning curiosity into an errand.
rumors.overheard_line: Bia lowers her voice near the canteen shade: the little radio song gets clear beside the locked storage room only when the afternoon clouds cover the courtyard.
rumors.source_behavior: She says it like proof, then changes 'storage room' to 'the other storage room' after Marina's keychain hisses with static.
rumors.follow_up_observation: The corridor door stays locked. The radio keychain gives one clean chorus, then only the canteen freezer hums.
